# Synthetic BIDS smoke fixture

A **deliberately empty** BIDS-format directory. Layout passes the BIDS
validator's structural rules (`dataset_description.json` present,
`participants.tsv` present, `sub-001/anat/sub-001_T1w.{nii.gz,json}`
present) so JOSS-style reviewers can run the Veritas smoke tests without
holding IDEAS / FCD / HS / AD credentials.

## Why it exists

The Veritas smoke-test path expects a BIDS subject directory it can hand
to MELD's `meld_prepare_bids_input.py`. Real subjects come from IDEAS or
a similar consortium with use agreements. For a reviewer who only wants
to confirm the platform compiles, boots, and accepts a request — this
synthetic subject is enough.

## Generate the placeholder NIfTI

The `sub-001_T1w.nii.gz` is generated by `scripts/generate_synthetic_bids_t1w.py`,
which is committed alongside this fixture. The script writes a
~200-byte gzip of a syntactically valid NIfTI-1 header with one voxel of
zero (no signal, no anatomy).

```bash
python3 scripts/generate_synthetic_bids_t1w.py \
  --output tests/fixtures/bids/sub-001/anat/sub-001_T1w.nii.gz
```

## What this fixture is NOT

- Not real anatomy. There is no signal.
- Not a substitute for IDEAS / FCD / HS data — those exist to make
  Veritas useful; this exists to make reviewers' lives easier.
- Not validated against FreeSurfer / MELD pipelines. The MELD
  preprocessing step will reject it. That's the correct outcome;
  the goal is to surface "your platform is reachable, your auth and
  audit work, your routing is fine" — not "MELD ran."
